task_description: Screen each abstract for a systematic review on ML methods for chest radiograph interpretation and pathology detection. INCLUDE a paper only if it presents or evaluates an ML model that detects, classifies, or localises one or more pathological findings from chest X-ray images. Papers about chest CT, MRI, PET, non-chest imaging, automated reporting without detection, or pure segmentation without classification should be EXCLUDE.

papers.title: DenseNet-121 for Multi-Label Chest X-Ray Pathology Detection on CheXpert
papers.abstract: We train DenseNet-121 on 224,000 frontal chest radiographs from the CheXpert dataset to detect 14 thoracic conditions including atelectasis, cardiomegaly, consolidation, pleural effusion, and pneumothorax. Mean AUC 0.88 across pathologies is achieved, outperforming CheXNet and a ResNet-50 baseline. Visualisation using Grad-CAM confirms the network attends to clinically expected regions.
papers.topic_hint: DenseNet, chest X-ray, CheXpert, multi-label pathology detection
papers.methodology_hint: DenseNet-121, CheXpert 224k, AUC 0.88, CheXNet and ResNet-50 baselines, Grad-CAM
papers.claimed_contribution: DenseNet-121 for 14-label CXR pathology detection AUC 0.88, 2 baselines, Grad-CAM.

papers.title: Contrastive Learning for Chest CT Nodule Detection at Low Radiation Dose
papers.abstract: Low-dose CT nodule detection benefits from contrastive pre-training to offset small labelled datasets. We apply SimCLR pre-training on 180,000 unlabelled chest CT scans and fine-tune for nodule detection. Sensitivity 0.92 at 1 false positive per scan is achieved on the LUNA16 benchmark. This work uses chest CT, not chest X-ray.
papers.topic_hint: contrastive learning, chest CT, nodule detection, LUNA16, low-dose
papers.methodology_hint: SimCLR, chest CT, LUNA16, sensitivity 0.92 — chest CT not chest X-ray
papers.claimed_contribution: SimCLR for CT nodule detection sensitivity 0.92, LUNA16, chest CT not CXR.

papers.title: Vision Transformer for Pneumonia Detection from Paediatric Chest Radiographs
papers.abstract: Pneumonia is a leading cause of childhood mortality. We fine-tune ViT-B/16 on 5,216 paediatric chest radiographs from the Kaggle chest X-ray dataset to classify normal versus pneumonia. AUC 0.96 and sensitivity 0.94 at specificity 0.92 are achieved, outperforming ResNet-50 and EfficientNet-B4 baselines.
papers.topic_hint: ViT, pneumonia detection, paediatric chest X-ray, Kaggle dataset
papers.methodology_hint: ViT-B/16, Kaggle chest X-ray 5216 images, AUC 0.96, ResNet-50 and EfficientNet baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: ViT for paediatric pneumonia detection AUC 0.96, 2 baselines.

papers.title: Automated Radiology Report Generation from Chest X-Rays Using GPT-4V
papers.abstract: Large vision-language models can generate free-text radiology reports from chest radiographs. We evaluate GPT-4V on chest X-ray report generation using MIMIC-CXR reference reports. ROUGE-L 0.41 and RadGraph F1 0.52 are achieved. The system generates narrative text; it does not classify pathology labels or produce structured detection outputs.
papers.topic_hint: GPT-4V, report generation, chest X-ray, MIMIC-CXR, radiology NLP
papers.methodology_hint: GPT-4V, MIMIC-CXR, ROUGE/RadGraph metrics — narrative generation not pathology detection
papers.claimed_contribution: GPT-4V for CXR report generation, ROUGE-L 0.41, narrative text not structured detection.

papers.title: EfficientNet for Tuberculosis Detection in Chest Radiographs from Low-Income Settings
papers.abstract: Chest X-ray remains the primary TB screening tool in low-income countries. We fine-tune EfficientNet-B2 on 10,000 frontal CXRs from Nigeria and India, achieving AUC 0.94 for TB detection, outperforming CAD4TB and a logistic regression with WHO clinical criteria. We evaluate specifically on low-quality digital radiographs from portable devices.
papers.topic_hint: EfficientNet, tuberculosis, chest X-ray, low-resource, detection
papers.methodology_hint: EfficientNet-B2, 10k CXRs Nigeria+India, AUC 0.94, CAD4TB and LR baselines, portable devices
papers.claimed_contribution: EfficientNet TB detection AUC 0.94 on portable CXRs, outperforming CAD4TB.

papers.title: U-Net for Cardiac Silhouette Segmentation in Chest Radiographs
papers.abstract: Accurate cardiac silhouette segmentation enables automated cardiothoracic ratio measurement for cardiomegaly screening. We train a U-Net on 1,800 annotated CXRs to segment the cardiac outline. Dice 0.94 is achieved. The model segments anatomy; it does not classify pathology or produce a diagnosis.
papers.topic_hint: U-Net, cardiac segmentation, chest X-ray, cardiothoracic ratio
papers.methodology_hint: U-Net, 1800 CXRs, Dice 0.94, cardiac segmentation — anatomy not pathology classification
papers.claimed_contribution: U-Net for cardiac segmentation from CXR, Dice 0.94, no pathology classification.

papers.title: Object Detection Network for Pneumothorax Localisation in Emergency Chest X-Rays
papers.abstract: Rapid pneumothorax detection in emergency settings is critical. We train a YOLOv8 model on 4,200 chest radiographs to detect and localise pneumothorax, outputting bounding box predictions. AUC 0.93 and intersection-over-union 0.71 are achieved on the SIIM Pneumothorax Segmentation dataset. Comparison against a segmentation approach and a classification baseline is provided.
papers.topic_hint: YOLOv8, pneumothorax, chest X-ray, localisation, emergency radiology
papers.methodology_hint: YOLOv8, SIIM dataset 4200 CXRs, AUC 0.93, IoU 0.71, segmentation and classification baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: YOLOv8 for pneumothorax localisation, AUC 0.93, IoU 0.71, 2 baselines.

papers.title: Federated Learning for COVID-19 Severity Grading from Chest CT Across 20 Hospitals
papers.abstract: COVID-19 CT severity grading requires large multi-site datasets. We implement federated learning across 20 hospitals to train a CNN for CT severity scoring without sharing patient images. AUC 0.91 is achieved. This work uses chest CT, not chest radiographs.
papers.topic_hint: federated learning, COVID-19, chest CT, severity grading, multi-site
papers.methodology_hint: federated CNN, chest CT 20 hospitals, AUC 0.91 — chest CT not chest X-ray
papers.claimed_contribution: Federated CNN for COVID-19 CT severity grading AUC 0.91, chest CT not CXR.

papers.title: Self-Supervised Pre-training on MIMIC-CXR for Downstream Pathology Detection
papers.abstract: Self-supervised pre-training on large unlabelled CXR datasets improves label efficiency. We apply DINO pre-training on 300,000 unlabelled frontal CXRs from MIMIC-CXR and fine-tune for detection of 12 thoracic conditions. Mean AUC 0.87 is achieved using only 1% of labelled training data, outperforming ImageNet pre-training and a fully supervised baseline on the same 1% labels.
papers.topic_hint: DINO, self-supervised, MIMIC-CXR, pathology detection, label-efficient
papers.methodology_hint: DINO pre-training, 300k CXRs, AUC 0.87 at 1% labels, ImageNet and supervised baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: DINO pre-training for CXR pathology detection AUC 0.87 with 1% labels, label-efficient.

papers.title: Multimodal Fusion of CXR Images and Clinical Notes for Pathology Classification
papers.abstract: Combining visual and textual information may improve automated diagnosis. We train a multimodal model fusing DenseNet image features and ClinicalBERT note embeddings from MIMIC-CXR to classify 14 chest pathologies. Mean AUC improves from 0.86 (image only) to 0.90 (multimodal). The model uses chest radiographs as the imaging modality.
papers.topic_hint: multimodal, CXR, clinical notes, ClinicalBERT, pathology classification
papers.methodology_hint: DenseNet + ClinicalBERT fusion, MIMIC-CXR, AUC 0.90 vs 0.86 image-only, CXR images
papers.claimed_contribution: Multimodal CXR+notes fusion for pathology detection AUC 0.90, outperforming image-only.

papers.title: Knowledge Distillation for Efficient Chest X-Ray Classification on Mobile Devices
papers.abstract: Deploying CXR AI on resource-limited devices requires model compression. We apply knowledge distillation from a DenseNet-121 teacher to a lightweight student network, achieving AUC 0.86 for 14-label CXR pathology detection at 4x faster inference and 5x fewer parameters. Comparison against the original DenseNet and a pruning baseline is provided.
papers.topic_hint: knowledge distillation, chest X-ray, efficient inference, mobile deployment
papers.methodology_hint: knowledge distillation, DenseNet-121 teacher, CXR 14-label, AUC 0.86, pruning baseline, CXR pathology
papers.claimed_contribution: Knowledge distillation for CXR pathology detection AUC 0.86 at 4x speedup, 2 baselines.

papers.title: Bayesian Neural Network for Pleural Effusion Quantification from Chest X-Ray with Uncertainty
papers.abstract: Pleural effusion grading guides clinical management decisions. We train a Bayesian CNN on 8,700 frontal CXRs to classify pleural effusion into none, small, moderate, and large. Quadratic weighted kappa 0.84 is achieved with calibrated confidence intervals. Predictions with high uncertainty are automatically flagged for radiologist review.
papers.topic_hint: Bayesian CNN, pleural effusion, chest X-ray, grading, uncertainty
papers.methodology_hint: Bayesian CNN, 8700 CXRs, kappa 0.84, uncertainty calibration, pleural effusion grading
papers.claimed_contribution: Bayesian CNN for pleural effusion grading kappa 0.84 with uncertainty flagging.

papers.title: Radiology Workflow Analysis Using Eye-Tracking to Optimise Display Protocols
papers.abstract: Understanding how radiologists read chest X-rays can improve display protocols and AI-assist positioning. We use eye-tracking to study reading patterns of 32 radiologists interpreting 400 CXRs, measuring dwell time and fixation counts per region. No automated pathology detection model is built or evaluated.
papers.topic_hint: eye-tracking, radiologist reading, workflow analysis, CXR display
papers.methodology_hint: eye-tracking study, 32 radiologists, reading patterns — workflow analysis not automated detection
papers.claimed_contribution: Eye-tracking study of CXR reading patterns for display optimisation, no automated detection.

papers.title: GAN-Based Chest X-Ray Synthesis for Augmenting Rare Pathology Training Data
papers.abstract: Rare pathologies like pulmonary embolism and aortic dissection are underrepresented in CXR datasets. We train a conditional GAN conditioned on pathology labels to synthesise realistic chest X-rays containing these findings. Classifiers trained with GAN-augmented data improve AUC for rare conditions by 8–11 points. This is a data augmentation method paper contributing to CXR pathology detection.
papers.topic_hint: GAN, CXR synthesis, data augmentation, rare pathology, chest X-ray
papers.methodology_hint: conditional GAN, CXR augmentation, AUC improvement 8-11 points, rare pathology CXR detection
papers.claimed_contribution: Conditional GAN CXR synthesis improving rare pathology detection AUC by 8-11 points.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Bone Mineral Density Estimation from Chest X-Rays
papers.abstract: Chest X-rays may encode information about bone mineral density useful for opportunistic osteoporosis screening. We train a CNN on 14,000 lateral chest radiographs with DXA-derived BMD labels to estimate bone mineral density. Pearson r=0.78 is achieved. This estimates a metabolic biomarker; it does not detect pulmonary or cardiac pathology.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, bone mineral density, chest X-ray, osteoporosis, opportunistic screening
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, 14k lateral CXRs, Pearson 0.78, BMD estimation — metabolic biomarker not pathology detection
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for BMD estimation from CXR, Pearson 0.78, opportunistic osteoporosis screening, not pathology.
